source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/04_제품별 인허가 진행 문서/05_Blue (G1417MCW 외 7종)/▶ 국내 제조인증/2. 250402 보완/기술문서_수정중/2. 첨부자료/2. Cybersecurity/A-CDR-BLU_Cybersecurity Development Report.docx

sourceSummary.oneLine: Cybersecurity Development Report for G Series Detector digital flat panel X-ray detector models documenting cybersecurity lifecycle activities and procedures.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Provides cybersecurity development, risk management, verification, update management, post-production monitoring, and incident response information for regulatory bodies and stakeholders.
keyPoints: The report states it documents activities and procedures to ensure cybersecurity of the G Series Detector in accordance with IEC 81001-5-1.
The stated purpose is to demonstrate compliance with IEC 81001-5-1 and support approval and certification processes.
Cybersecurity risk identification references ISO 14971:2019, TR 24971:2020, AAMI TIR 57, FDA guidance, NIST 800-30, STRIDE, CVSS, and CWE.

The document identifies production and post-production cybersecurity activities including CISA monitoring, CVD participation, patching, release documentation, file integrity, and secure disposal guidance.
Updates cannot be performed directly in the field and must be performed by returning the product to the manufacturer or service center.

entities.name: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector
entities.evidence: Product name: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

entities.name: G Series Detector
entities.evidence: The Cybersecurity Development Report for the G Series Detector documents all the activities and procedures taken to ensure the cybersecurity of this specific device

entities.name: R&D
entities.evidence: R&D Manager 1) Approval of Cyber Security Risk Management

entities.name: RA
entities.evidence: RA 1) Conducting and managing third-party testing related to cybersecurity testing

entities.name: Quality Control
entities.evidence: Quality Control QMR 1) Monitoring cyber hazards and reporting to regulators

entities.name: CS Team
entities.evidence: When a cyber issue arises in the market, the CS Team checks the situation on the spot, prepares a customer complaint report, and delivers the contents to the research institute.

concepts.name: Cybersecurity development lifecycle
concepts.evidence: This report includes detailed records of architecture analysis, threat modeling, vulnerability analysis, identification of security requirements, risk assessment, and mitigation.

concepts.name: Threat modeling
concepts.evidence: Threat modeling is the process of systematically identifying potential threats and vulnerabilities that could affect a medical device.

concepts.name: STRIDE
concepts.evidence: Techniques such as STRIDE (Spoofing, Tampering, Repudiation, Information Disclosure, Denial of Service, Elevation of Privilege) can be used to categorize and analyze threats.

concepts.name: Cybersecurity risk management
concepts.evidence: Managing cybersecurity risks for G Series Detector starts with reviewing the completed or updated risk management processes at every design phase.

concepts.name: Software Bill of Materials
concepts.evidence: Managing an SBOM (Software Bill of Materials) for medical device cybersecurity involves creating a detailed inventory of all software components, continuously monitoring for vulnerabilities.

concepts.name: Continuous monitoring
concepts.evidence: Continuous monitoring is essential in this approach and includes regular vulnerability scans, static application security testing analysis, and internal reviews to identify and address security risks.

concepts.name: Update management
concepts.evidence: Following guidelines from the FDA and NIST, update management includes identifying, acquiring, testing, and deploying software updates to fix vulnerabilities or enhance functionality.

concepts.name: Security incident response
concepts.evidence: The Security Incident Response Plan outlines a structured approach for handling cybersecurity incidents related to G SERIES DETECTOR.

concepts.name: Coordinated Vulnerability Disclosure
concepts.evidence: H&abyz plans to actively participate in the CVD process to monitor the Cybersecurity Vulnerability report issued by CISA or CERT and apply the patch, update, and other mitigation related to the Vulnerability if necessary.

concepts.name: MDS2
concepts.pageTarget: Manufacturer Disclosure Statement for Medical Device Security
concepts.evidence: To verify interoperability functionality, we have added the MDS2 (Manufacturer Disclosure Statement for Medical Device Security) checklist.

evidence.claim: The document is a Cybersecurity Development Report for the G Series Detector.
evidence.evidenceText: The Cybersecurity Development Report for the G Series Detector documents all the activities and procedures taken to ensure the cybersecurity of this specific device

evidence.claim: The product is a Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector.
evidence.evidenceText: Product name: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

evidence.claim: The covered models are G1417CW, G1717CW, G1417FW, G1717FW, G1417CWP, G1717CWP, G1417FWP, and G1717FWP.
evidence.evidenceText: Model name: G1417CW/G1717CW/G1417FW/G1717FW/G1417CWP/G1717CWP/G1417FWP/G1717FWP

evidence.claim: The manufacturer is H&abyz Co., Ltd.
evidence.evidenceText: Manufacturer H&abyz Co., Ltd.

evidence.claim: The report revision is Revision No. 0 with initial revision date December 05, 2024.
evidence.evidenceText: Revision No. 0 Rev. Date December 05, 2024 Revision Description Initial

evidence.claim: The report was prepared, reviewed, and approved on December 05, 2024.
evidence.evidenceText: Prepared by Han, Jimin R&D team December 05, 2024 Reviewed by Hur, Namkyu Quality Management Representative December 05, 2024 Approved by Jeon, Jeu R&D manager December 05, 2024

evidence.claim: The purpose of the report is to demonstrate compliance with IEC 81001-5-1.
evidence.evidenceText: The purpose of the Cybersecurity Development Report for G Series Detector is to demonstrate compliance with the IEC 81001-5-1 standard

evidence.claim: The report supports approval and certification by providing cybersecurity information to regulatory bodies and stakeholders.
evidence.evidenceText: the report provides precise and reliable cybersecurity information to regulatory bodies and other stakeholders, facilitating the approval and certification process

evidence.claim: The device is intended for screening and diagnosis by qualified or trained doctors or technicians on adult subjects for diagnostic radiographic exposures.
evidence.evidenceText: G1417/G1717 Series are digital flat panel detectors that is used for screening and diagnosis of disease of injury. These detectors are intended for use by a qualified/trained doctor or technician on both adult subjects for taking diagnostic radiographic exposures

evidence.claim: The detectors are not intended for mammography applications.
evidence.evidenceText: These detectors are not intended to be used for mammography applications.

evidence.claim: The operational environment identifies AXD software version Ver 1.0.0 and no off-the-shelf software.
evidence.evidenceText: Off-the-shelf: None Software name: AXD Version: Ver 1.0.0

evidence.claim: Security requirements are derived based on IEC 60601-4-5.
evidence.evidenceText: This involves identifying both functional and non-functional security requirements based on the IEC 60601-4-5.

evidence.claim: Cybersecurity risks are identified, analyzed, and evaluated with reference to ISO 14971:2019, TR 24971:2020, AAMI TIR 57, FDA guidelines, and other international guidelines.
evidence.evidenceText: This will be done with reference to ISO 14971:2019, TR 24971:2020, AAMI TIR 57 standards, and the latest international guidelines, including those from the FDA.

evidence.claim: Risk evaluation follows NIST 800-30 and ISO 14971:2019 and classifies critical, high, and medium risks as unacceptable.
evidence.evidenceText: risk evaluation follows the NIST 800-30 guideline and ISO 14971:2019 standard. This is done by multiplying the impact and likelihood to classify risk levels. The levels include, critical, high, medium, low, and very low, and they determine the acceptability of the risk. Risks at critical, High and Medium levels are deemed unacceptable

evidence.claim: Verification activities include SAST, requirement testing, vulnerability testing, fuzzing testing, and penetration testing.
evidence.evidenceText: Verification activities (Example) Test Description Static Application Security Testing (SAST) Requirement testing Vulnerability testing Fuzzing testing Penetration testing

evidence.claim: H&abyz plans quarterly CISA catalog monitoring by cybersecurity officers to identify cyber risks.
evidence.evidenceText: H&abyz monitors the CISA catalog by cybersecurity officers within the quarterly one-time lab to identify cyber risks associated with H&abyz's harm.

evidence.claim: Confirmed vulnerabilities must be reported to government agencies such as the U.S. DHS ICS-CERT program.
evidence.evidenceText: Confirmed vulnerabilities must result in a direct report into government agencies such as the U.S. DHS (ICS-CERT program)

evidence.claim: Update targets include firmware, security patches, and configuration files.
evidence.evidenceText: Update targets for medical device cybersecurity include software components, firmware, security patches, and configuration files

evidence.claim: Updates cannot be performed directly in the field and require return to the manufacturer or service center.
evidence.evidenceText: Updates cannot be performed directly in the field; you must return the product to the manufacturer or service center to perform the update.

evidence.claim: The incident response plan follows FDA and NIST guidelines and includes preparation, detection and analysis, containment, eradication and recovery, and post-incident activities.
evidence.evidenceText: It has been developed in accordance with FDA and NIST guidelines... The plan incorporates the Incident Response Life Cycle, which includes preparation, detection and analysis, containment, eradication and recovery, and post-incident activities

reviewFlags.reason: Source metadata authoritySignal is MFDS/domestic, but the source text does not explicitly mention MFDS; the document text primarily references FDA, NIST, CISA, DHS ICS-CERT, and CERT.
The source contains conflicting CVSS version references: vulnerability evaluation states CVSS 3.1, while the risk estimation section states NVD CVSS c3 Calculator / CVSS version 3.0.

OCR or text extraction quality issues are present, including apparent typos such as 'CVSS c3 Calculator', 'Unresolved Animals issue', duplicated 'standards required for safe and secure operation', and spacing artifacts.
No formal document identifier is provided beyond the source file name and title.
